name: Preserve flag on routes
description: Add a 'preserved' boolean column on routes that the cleanup worker will respect (preserved routes are never auto-deleted). Expose an API to toggle it. Modeled after comma connect's 'preserve' behavior. Frontend wiring (a star/bookmark button on the route detail page) is out of scope here -- this feature is schema + API only.
acceptance_criteria: sql/migrations/004_route_preserved.up.sql adds: ALTER TABLE routes ADD COLUMN preserved BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false; plus an index WHERE preserved = true to keep preserved-lookup fast
sql/queries/routes.sql adds SetRoutePreserved(dongle_id, route_name, preserved) and ListPreservedRoutes queries; sqlc generate produces matching Go
PUT /v1/routes/:dongle_id/:route_name/preserved with JSON body { "preserved": true|false } sets the flag (requires JWT auth); returns the updated route row
GET /v1/route/:dongle_id response (existing list endpoint) now includes 'preserved' on each route in the JSON
internal/api/route_test.go adds: set-preserved happy path, set-preserved on nonexistent route -> 404, list includes preserved field
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
notes: Schema: added routes.preserved column (migration 004) with partial index on dongle_id WHERE preserved = true. Queries: SetRoutePreserved + ListPreservedRoutes via sqlc. API: PUT /v1/routes/:dongle_id/:route_name/preserved; GET /v1/route/:dongle_id and /v1/route/:dongle_id/:route_name both include 'preserved' field. Tests cover happy path, 404, forbidden, bad auth, and DB error. Cleanup-worker wiring is a separate feature and not in scope here.
